title: Landolphia glabra
taxonomy: Landolphia glabra is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Apocynaceae. This plant belongs to the order Gentianales and is further classified under the genus Landolphia. It is native to tropical Africa and is also known by the name Yellow Stink Ball. The species Landolphia glabra is a type of evergreen shrub or small tree. Its classification is further supported by its unique characteristics and growth habits.
uses: Landolphia glabra has been traditionally used in various medical applications, with its extracts being utilized for treating different health conditions. Its roots are said to have antimicrobial properties, which may be beneficial in treating infections. The plant is also believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential treatment for reducing swelling and pain. Additionally, its extracts have been used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Landolphia glabra for medicinal purposes.

These preparations are believed to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects.
